pr.title: lb: Match lbbgflash functions + ftColl partial
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Unmerged duplicate PR that attempted to match two lbbgflash functions, lbBgFlash_8002063C and fn_80021C80, and partially implement ftColl_8007BE3C at 89.2%, while also adding many prototype and matching-status changes across fighter, stage, item, game-mode, and library files. The PR was closed because the same lbbgflash, ftColl_8007BE3C, and fn_80021C80 content had already been merged in PR #2013. Evidence for the named lbbgflash work comes from the PR body and closing comment; the provided changed-files list and diff excerpt do not show src/melee/lb/lbbgflash.c.
what_changed: PR body claimed 100% matches for lbBgFlash_8002063C and fn_80021C80 in lbbgflash.c.
PR body claimed a partial implementation of ftColl_8007BE3C with an 89.2% match.

Added or refined function prototypes to address CI/build issues, including replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with typed signatures such as s32 ftCo_800D7268(void*) and s32 ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8(HSD_GObj*).
Changed multiple configure.py entries from NonMatching to Matching, including ftcoll.c, ft_0892.c, ftCo_Guard.c, ftCo_Attack100.c, ftYs_SpecialS.c, ftCh_Init.c, and grcorneria.c.

Implemented several previously stubbed fighter functions in the diff excerpt, including ftCo_800D7268, fn_800D7830, fn_800D7BDC, ftCo_CatchDash_Phys, ftCh_Init_80155FCC, fn_80157080, ftCh_Damage2_Phys, ftCh_BackCrush_Anim, ftCh_BackDisappear_Anim, ftCh_Cancel_Anim, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11AC, and a static Kirby helper fn_800F1CA0.
Added matching aids in fighter code such as PAD_STACK(8), M2C_FIELD accesses, explicit callback assignments, and raw offset casts for not-yet-fully-typed Fighter fields.
Updated ftcoll.dox to type ftColl_80077464 as void ftColl_80077464(Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*) instead of an unknown signature.

Added a new CLAUDE.md repository guidance file describing project architecture, build commands, decompilation workflow, naming conventions, struct offset field style, literal formatting, and common abbreviations.
Closed without merge because the content was reported as already merged in PR #2013.
smart_moves: The PR connected implementation work to missing prototype fixes, which is important because matched functions often expose previously unknown cross-TU signatures needed by CI.
Typed headers were updated alongside call sites, reducing reliance on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and making future matching work less ambiguous.

The diff used known decomp matching tactics such as PAD_STACK for stack layout, M2C_FIELD for unresolved struct fields, and callback assignment through exact offsets when full type recovery was not available.

Several functions were implemented as small control-flow-faithful translations rather than over-abstracted rewrites, for example switch-based boolean filtering in fn_800F1CA0 and frame-remaining checks that call the next state callback.
The closing comment correctly avoided duplicating already-merged work by identifying PR #2013 as the source of the same lbbgflash and ftColl content.
decomp_lessons: Before opening or reviewing a generated matching PR, search recent merged PRs for the same function symbols; this PR was closed because fn_80021C80, ftColl_8007BE3C, and lbbgflash work had already landed in PR #2013.

Treat PR-body claims cautiously when the changed-files list does not include the named source file; here, lbbgflash.c was central to the title/body but absent from the provided changed-files list and diff excerpt.
When moving an object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, ensure the corresponding source diff actually supports that status and is not piggybacking unrelated matches.
Prototype recovery can be as important as function body matching: replacing unknown prototypes can unblock CI and expose real dependencies between fighter, item, and collision code.
Use temporary raw offset accessors such as M2C_FIELD only when type information is not recovered yet, and prefer later follow-up work to move those offsets into named struct fields.
Small stack-layout nudges like PAD_STACK should be documented or localized because they are matching tactics rather than semantic code.
Avoid committing local agent guidance files such as CLAUDE.md unless the project intentionally wants them; the diff added one, but no review evidence confirms it was desired.
naming_conventions: Library functions in this slice use the lb prefix, e.g. lbBgFlash_8002063C and lbAudioAx_80028B90.
Fighter collision functions use the ftColl_ address-based naming pattern while still unknown or partially recovered, e.g. ftColl_8007BE3C and ftColl_80077464.
Common fighter action code uses ftCo_ prefixes, e.g. ftCo_Attack100, ftCo_Guard, ftCo_CatchDash_Phys, and ftCo_800D7268.
Character-specific fighter code uses two-letter character abbreviations under ft/chara, e.g. ftCh for Crazy Hand, ftKb for Kirby, and ftPp for Popo.
Stage files use gr prefixes such as grcorneria, grzebes, gronett, grbigblue, and grrcruise.
Headers in the diff preserve address comments before prototypes, such as /* 0D7268 */ and /* 15A184 */.

The added CLAUDE.md documented intended style: function prefix matches filename, struct fields use hex-offset prefixes like x0_ and x10_, floats use uppercase F suffixes, and NULL checks should be explicit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_Guard_Anim and ftCo_GuardOff_Anim to influence stack layout for matching.
Used M2C_FIELD for unresolved Fighter struct accesses at offsets such as 0x10, 0x894, 0x89C, 0x21DC, 0x2238, and 0x2344.
Used exact floating literal style such as 0.0F and 1.0F in newly implemented functions.
Preserved callback wiring through fields like accessory4_cb and take_dmg_cb to match state-machine behavior.
Used raw pointer arithmetic and casts for Crazy Hand data around offsets 0x234C and 0x2358 where struct fields were not yet named.
Used explicit switch cases in fn_800F1CA0 instead of a more compact expression, likely to preserve compiler output shape.
Updated configure.py matching status entries after source implementations, though the PR was not merged.
review_feedback: No line review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
Issue comment stated: "Closing as duplicate - the content (fn_80021C80, ftColl_8007BE3C, lbbgflash functions) was already merged in PR #2013."
The PR was closed unmerged, so its useful decomp work should be traced through PR #2013 rather than reused directly from this branch.
Evidence is weak for the exact lbbgflash.c diff because the provided changed-files list and diff excerpt do not include lbbgflash.c despite the title and body naming it.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2013 for the authoritative merged implementation of lbBgFlash_8002063C, fn_80021C80, and ftColl_8007BE3C.
Verify whether src/melee/lb/lbbgflash.c was present in the full PR diff or omitted from this slice.
Check whether the configure.py NonMatching-to-Matching flips in this duplicate PR correspond to already-merged source changes.
Audit remaining M2C_FIELD offsets in ftCo_Attack100, ftCh_Init, and ftKb_Init for opportunities to name Fighter struct fields.
Confirm whether CLAUDE.md was ever intentionally accepted into the repository or was only an artifact of generated-agent workflow.
